text: A developer wants to change the color of a button to red. Why might the button still appear blue, despite the new CSS rule?

options.A: Inline styles have higher specificity than ID selectors.
options.B: The `.button-class` rule is defined in a separate file.
options.C: The `#myButton` rule has higher specificity than the class selector.
options.D: The `!important` keyword might be used elsewhere.

text: What will be logged to the console when this JavaScript code executes?
A developer is fetching data but wants to handle potential network errors gracefully. Which of the following approaches correctly handles errors in an `async/await` function?

options.A: The `try...catch` block correctly catches network errors.
options.B: Errors from `response.json()` would also be caught.
options.C: Errors from `fetch()` itself are caught by the `catch` block.
options.D: The `throw error;` ensures the error propagates for further handling.

options.D: The code will throw an error.

text: What is the correct order of console logs for this JavaScript code?
A React component's state is not updating as expected. Which of these could be a reason for the incorrect behavior?

options.A: Directly modifying the `count` variable instead of using `setCount`'s functional update.
options.B: The `setCount` call is asynchronous and might not reflect immediately.
options.C: The component is not re-rendering because `count` is a primitive value.
options.D: The `handleClick` function is not memoized, causing re-renders.

text: When should you return a cleanup function from a `useEffect` hook in React?

options.A: Only when the effect has no dependencies.
options.B: To prevent memory leaks from subscriptions or timers.
options.C: When you want to run code after every render.

text: You have a large, complex React component tree where a prop (e.g., a theme object) is passed down through many intermediate components. What are effective ways to avoid 'prop drilling' and improve performance?

options.A: Use the React Context API to provide the theme.
options.B: Lift the state up to a common ancestor component.
options.C: Use `React.memo` on intermediate components to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
options.D: Pass the theme as a global variable.

text: What will be the output when `main.js` is executed?

options.D: Error: utils.add is not a function

text: A Node.js Express route handler performs an asynchronous database operation. Which practices help prevent unhandled promise rejections or server crashes?

options.A: Wrap the `await` call in a `try...catch` block.
options.B: Add a `.catch()` handler to the promise chain if not using `await`.
options.C: Implement a global error handling middleware in Express.
options.D: Use `process.on('unhandledRejection', ...)` to log unhandled promise rejections.

text: What will be the output in the console when a GET request is made to `/test`?
A Node.js Express application needs a centralized way to catch and respond to all errors. What is the best practice for implementing this?

options.A: Add `try...catch` blocks in every route handler.
options.C: Define an Express middleware with four arguments (`err, req, res, next`).
options.D: Wrap the entire Express app in a single `try...catch` block.

options.A: Manually edit the conflicted files to choose the desired changes.
